name: Wu Zixu
description: Wu Zixu, also known as Wu Yun, was a prominent figure in Chinese history during the late Spring and Autumn period (771 to 476 BC). Born in the state of Chu, his life was marked by tragedy, loyalty, and vengeance. He is revered as a symbol of unwavering loyalty, filial piety, and strategic brilliance. Wu Zixu's father and elder brother were unjustly executed by the King of Chu, forcing him to flee to the state of Wu. There, he gained the trust of Prince Guang, who later became King Helü of Wu. Wu Zixu played a pivotal role in transforming Wu into a powerful kingdom, advising King Helü on military strategy and state affairs. He oversaw the construction of the 'Great City' of Suzhou, which became the Wu capital. He also recommended Sun Tzu, the author of 'The Art of War', to King Helü, significantly strengthening Wu's military prowess. After King Helü's death, Wu Zixu served his successor, King Fuchai. However, he warned Fuchai against trusting Goujian, the King of Yue, whom Wu had previously defeated. Tragically, Fuchai, blinded by ambition and influenced by treacherous advisors, ignored Wu Zixu's warnings and eventually forced him to commit suicide. Before his death, Wu Zixu prophesied Wu's downfall, requesting that his eyes be placed on the city gates to witness the Yue army's conquest. His prophecy came true, solidifying his legacy as a tragic hero and a symbol of unwavering loyalty and foresight.
subtitle: Loyalty, Vengeance, and Strategic Brilliance
achievements: Advising King Helü to transform Wu into a powerful kingdom
Oversaw the construction of the Great City of Suzhou
Recommending Sun Tzu to King Helü
Aiding Wu in defeating the state of Chu
Prophesying the downfall of Wu due to Fuchai's betrayal

keyMyths.title: Flight from Chu
keyMyths.description: After his father and brother were killed by the King of Chu, Wu Zixu fled to Wu, facing numerous hardships and demonstrating his unwavering determination to avenge his family.

keyMyths.title: Recommendation of Sun Tzu
keyMyths.description: Wu Zixu recognized Sun Tzu's military genius and recommended him to King Helü, leading to the creation of a formidable Wu army and the writing of 'The Art of War'.
keyMyths.source: The Art of War by Sun Tzu

keyMyths.title: Wu Zixu's Suicide
keyMyths.description: Falsely accused of treason, Wu Zixu was forced to commit suicide by King Fuchai. Before his death, he predicted Wu's downfall and requested his eyes be placed on the city gates to witness it.

sources.author: Sima Qian
sources.description: A comprehensive history of China covering the period from the Yellow Emperor to the Han dynasty, providing detailed accounts of Wu Zixu's life and deeds.
sources.text: A comprehensive history of China covering the period from the Yellow Emperor to the Han dynasty, providing detailed accounts of Wu Zixu's life and deeds.

sources.author: Sun Tzu
sources.description: A seminal work on military strategy, influenced by Wu Zixu's understanding of warfare and his recommendation of Sun Tzu to King Helü.
sources.text: A seminal work on military strategy, influenced by Wu Zixu's understanding of warfare and his recommendation of Sun Tzu to King Helü.
